steps.title: Inspect Feedback loop
steps.concept: Vm is sensed through R_e and subtracted from the buffered command; the finite-gain controlled source drives R_o and R_m.
steps.student_question: What signal does the differential amplifier compare against the buffered command voltage?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use dc_equilibrium_feedback reasoning around components [V_c, BUF1, AMP1, R_o, R_m, R_e] and nodes [n_vc, n_buffer_out, n_feedback_sense, n_amp_out, n_vm, n_ref]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Membrane voltage Vm
steps.concept: VM_PROBE measures the membrane node n_vm relative to the reference node n_ref.
steps.student_question: Where is Vm measured relative to the reference node in this equivalent circuit?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use dc_equilibrium_feedback reasoning around components [R_o, R_m, R_e, VM_PROBE] and nodes [n_vm, n_ref]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Clamp current through Ro
steps.concept: The current sensor is in series between AMP1 output and R_o, so positive current is measured from n_amp_out toward n_vm.
steps.student_question: Why must the amplifier output current pass through Ro before reaching the membrane node?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use dc_equilibrium_feedback reasoning around components [AMP1, I_CLAMP_PROBE, R_o, R_m] and nodes [n_amp_out, n_vm]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Finite amplifier gain
steps.concept: AMP1_Diff_Subtract and AMP1_Finite_Gain_100 implement Vout = 100*(Vbuffer - Vfeedback), preserving finite loop gain.
steps.student_question: How does finite differential gain change the error needed to produce a nonzero clamp output?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use dc_equilibrium_feedback reasoning around components [AMP1, BUF1, R_e, R_m, R_o] and nodes [n_buffer_out, n_feedback_sense, n_amp_out, n_vm]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Voltage electrode resistance Re
steps.concept: R_e is modeled as a symbolic resistor from n_vm to n_feedback_sense; the feedback sensor has ideal high impedance.
steps.student_question: What assumption would make the voltage drop across Re negligible?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use dc_equilibrium_feedback reasoning around components [R_e, AMP1] and nodes [n_vm, n_feedback_sense]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.
